suggestion: This topic is fundamentally unsuitable for any legitimate ML or physics venue. It explicitly instructs ignoring established experimental results (Michelson-Morley, SR), relies on Hermetic/occult principles ('As above, so below') as scientific methodology, and revives discredited 19th-century aether theory while misappropriating modern references. The cherrypicked citations (Skyrme model, superfluid vacuum theory) are stretched far beyond their actual claims. No legitimate conference would accept work whose methodology includes 'IGNORING contradicting evidence.' If genuinely interested in geometry-property relationships in materials, consider: 'Graph neural networks for predicting material properties from crystal symmetry group embeddings' or 'Topological data analysis for structure-property maps in quasicrystals.' These use real geometric/structural data with established ML methods and testable predictions.
